mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as borrowing costs fall and risk appetite expands.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, it signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in negative earnings revisions and driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with falling federal funds rates indicate a steepening yield curve driven by growth expectations rather than inflation fears, generating positive equity returns as corporate borrowing costs stabilize while demand expands.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when RSAFS and FEDFUNDS move in the same direction, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term momentum buying that pushes S&P 500 returns positive over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during recovery phases, with signal-weighted portfolio Sharpe ratio falling below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP consistently correlates with corporate earnings growth, providing a persistent positive drift to S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter regardless of transient market sentiment shifts.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaches 215.0, it triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as discount rates rise sharply.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of periods following threshold breach, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, creating a positive feedback loop that drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of occurrences when series decouple, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, any upward tick in the unemployment rate signals labor market softening and peak earnings, prompting institutional deleveraging that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the mechanism shows no regime-dependent alpha.
